lede: A look at April 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ziyu He and Aaron Cunningham, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Provisional Irish Republican Army
events.desc: An IRA bomb devastates the Bishopsgate area of London.

events.title: Gruinard Island
events.desc: Gruinard Island, Scotland, is officially declared free of the anthrax disease after 48 years of quarantine.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The SBS launches a raid against the garrison of Santorini in Greece.

events.title: Nazi Germany
events.desc: Nazi Germany begins its persecution of Jehovah's Witnesses by shutting down the Watch Tower Society office in Magdeburg.

events.title: Imperial Wireless Chain
events.desc: The first segment of the Imperial Wireless Chain providing wireless telegraphy between Leafield in Oxfordshire, England, and Cairo, Egypt, comes into operation.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: First tank-to-tank combat, during the second Battle of Villers-Bretonneux. Three British Mark IVs meet three German A7Vs.

events.title: Franck–Hertz experiment
events.desc: The Franck–Hertz experiment, a pillar of quantum mechanics, is presented to the German Physical Society.

events.title: Woolworth Building
events.desc: The Woolworth Building, a skyscraper in New York City, is opened.

events.title: 1837 Surat fire
events.desc: The great fire in Surat, India causes more than 500 deaths and destruction of more than 9,000 houses.

events.title: Library of Congress
events.desc: The United States Library of Congress is established when President John Adams signs legislation to appropriate $5,000 to purchase "such books as may be necessary for the use of Congress".

births.name: Billy Gould
births.line: American bass player, songwriter, and producer

births.name: Phil Watson
births.line: Canadian ice hockey player and coach (died 1991)

deaths.text: Dirk Kempthorne, American businessman and politician, 49th United States Secretary of the Interior
Willa Cather, American novelist, short story writer, and poet
Axel von Fersen the Elder, Swedish field marshal and politician
